stories.headline: Fourth of July Fireworks in Cincinnati: Burrow Hitches Up Under Center
stories.read: Well pardner, the Bengals spent all spring pullin' Joe Burrow out of that shotgun hammock and puttin' him under center, where the play-action fake shakes loose the long ball. He's healthy again after that busted toe kept him laid up most of last season, and he's still slingin' to the two finest pass-catchers this side of the Mississippi in Ja'Marr Chase and Tee Higgins. If the saloon's still pricin' Burrow like damaged goods, that's cheap gold, and the whole Cincinnati wagon train rides higher with him.

stories.headline: Detroit Swears Jameson Williams Is Havin' the Best Summer of His Life
stories.read: The Lions' receiver boss Scottie Montgomery says this has been by far Jamo's best offseason, with the young burner sweatin' over his drops, his strength, and the detail it takes to go from good to truly elite. That's the kind of talk a prospector hears right before a vein gushes. Amon-Ra keeps the biggest cut of the throws, sure, but Williams is a deep-strike man growin' himself a floor, so if the herd's still grabbin' him like a third wheel, ride in and stake that claim early.

stories.headline: Seattle Fixin' to Hand Rashid Shaheed a Bigger Share of the Haul
stories.read: Word off the wire is Shaheed had himself a major spring, he and Sam Darnold are hittin' it off, and the new trail boss callin' plays aims to feed him underneath work instead of just the occasional moonshot downfield. More routes means more of the haul, plain and simple. Smith-Njigba's still the lead horse in that stable, but Shaheed's a vein the rest of the outfit ain't even struck yet. Pan that creek late in your land grab and tip your hat to ol' Doc come October.
